summary: Documentation-only PR adding a GitHub pull request template that points contributors to CONTRIBUTING.md, and replacing the prior Discord PR-notification note in CONTRIBUTING.md with AI assistance guidelines for matching work.

key_files.role: Updated contributor documentation with an AI Assistance section covering acceptable AI use, naming restrictions, data-section matching discouragement, and PR/comment etiquette for AI output.
New one-line PR template directing submitters to read the contributing guidelines before submitting.

what_changed.text: Added an `AI Assistance` section to `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` stating that using AI to match functions is acceptable and frequently practiced.
Added guidance to refrain from allowing AI to name struct fields or other globally accessible identifiers.
Added guidance to discourage AI from attempting to match data sections and to focus AI-assisted work solely on code matches.
Added guidance to avoid including AI output in PR or issue bodies/comments and to avoid automated code reviews, with a narrow allowance for relevant concise AI summaries wrapped as markdown comments.
Created `.github/pull_request_template.md` with a link to the contributing guidelines.
Removed the previous line stating that opening a PR notifies the `#smash-bros-melee` Discord channel.

smart_moves.text: Centralized PR-submission guidance by adding a pull request template that links back to CONTRIBUTING.md rather than duplicating process rules in the template.
Scoped AI guidance to concrete high-risk areas: global naming, data-section matching, and review/PR communication noise.

decomp_lessons.text: AI assistance is acceptable for function matching, but the PR evidence frames it as something to constrain around source-ownership and reviewability risks.
For AI-assisted matching, code matches are the intended focus; data-section matching should be discouraged unless separately justified.

naming_conventions.text: Do not let AI name struct fields or other globally accessible identifiers.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: When using AI, direct it toward function/code matching rather than data-section matching.

follow_up_queries: Refrain from allowing AI to name fields in structs
Discourage AI from attempting to match data sections
avoid including AI output in PR body comments automated code reviews

notes: The diff is small, complete, and directly supports the changed-file and guideline claims.
There were no issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the supplied slice, so no reviewer feedback or reviewer intent is inferred.
The raw PR JSON is truncated, but the loaded metadata and raw diff contain the fields needed for this intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: AI assistance is allowed for matching functions with guardrails
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2513 documents that AI use for matching functions is acceptable and frequently practiced, but should be constrained around naming, data-section matching, and PR/review communication.

accepted_candidate_records.title: AI should not name global identifiers or struct fields
accepted_candidate_records.text: For AI-assisted work, contributors are told to refrain from allowing AI to name fields in structs or other globally accessible identifiers.

accepted_candidate_records.title: AI-assisted work should focus on code matches rather than data sections
accepted_candidate_records.text: The added guidelines discourage AI from attempting to match data sections and instead focus AI assistance solely on code matches.

source_update_candidates.title: AI-assisted matching guardrails
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or cross-linking an AI-specific global standard: AI may be used to assist with function/code matching, but should not be treated as authoritative for naming struct fields or globally accessible identifiers, should not be used to chase data-section matches, and should not generate PR/review discussion except for concise clearly marked summaries when appropriate.
source_update_candidates.reason: The merged CONTRIBUTING.md update is source evidence of project guidance; curator may decide whether to mirror this as a decomp standard or leave it as repository contribution documentation.

rejection_notes: Do not infer any code-match validation, build status, or objdiff result from this PR; it only changes documentation.
Do not infer reviewer rationale because the supplied slice has zero reviews and zero review comments.
Do not generalize the AI guidance beyond the exact areas documented: function/code matching, naming of fields/global identifiers, data-section matching, and PR/comment/review output etiquette.
